fault_hypothesis: The function incorrectly removes the last element from the result if both input lists share the same last element. This is caused by the `merged.pop()` call on line 80, which is guarded by a faulty condition on line 79.

constraints.intent: The length of the merged list should be the sum of input lengths, which this line violates.
This branch, which incorrectly removes a shared last element, should never be executed.

spec.expr: not (left and right and left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition that triggers the faulty element removal should not be true before the check.
The final merged list must contain all elements from both input lists, preserving duplicates.
